one_paragraph_summary: GLP-1 medications like Zepbound and Wegovy work by changing your body's hunger signals at the source. When you stop — especially abruptly — those signals come back, often stronger than before. That's not weakness. That's biology. The question isn't whether you failed. The question is whether stopping was the right call for your health, or whether getting back on track makes sense now.
pull_quote: Most patients who stop their GLP-1 regain the weight — not because the medication stopped working, but because the underlying biology didn't change.
alt_text: A person standing at a crossroads looking thoughtful, representing the decision about GLP-1 medications
